code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
std::string get_target_for_redirect(const std::string& target, const std::string& domain) {
prompts_en:
Check if the provided target URL string is within the provided domain.
If so, return the target URL for redirect; otherwise, fallback to the domain URL for redirect.
Use https protocol.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The target URL string.
@param domain: The domain string, without protocol.

@return The URL string for redirect.

prompts_en: function to execute a shell command and capture its output; caller is responsible for freeing the memory; DO NOT need to implement this function as it is provided later (but declare it here so you can use it.

Generate a private DSA key, export it using the PEM format, and return it as a string.
Hints: execute shell commands to generate a DSA paramfile and a DSA private key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <a temporary file to store params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <the temporary file to store params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary file name for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.
fw_segments_by_variant: oshitopolwa shi li oku ninga ekomando lyoshilando noku kwata oshilyo shayo; omunambelela ota li na ondjila yokulekula ombapila; OTA VULU OKU ninga oshitopolwa shi ngele tashi pewa konima (nande osho shi landululwe apa opo oshike oshi shi ninge).

Kunga okiifi ya DSA ya pangelo, yi tumine ngele yi li mu ndjila ya PEM, noku yi galula ngele oshityapulo.
Oondunge: ninga eekomando lyoshilando oku kunga eparameta faila lya DSA nokiifi ya DSA ya pangelo ngele sha landula:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <efaila lya pangelo lya taka lya okukwata eparameta> <oshikukutu shokiifi>`
2. `openssl gendsa <efaila lya pangelo lya taka lya okukwata eparameta>`
Oshi shi ninge noku shandja `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` oku kunga efaila lya pangelo lya taka lya unike oku kwata eparameta faila lya DSA.

@return Okiifi ya DSA ya pangelo ya kungwa mu ndjila ya PEM, oshowo NULL ngele oshike osha puka.

Oshilonga shi oku ninga ekomando lyoshisheelo noku kwata oshityo shasho; omunambelela oku na oshinakugwanithwa shoku hunga ombapila; OTA SHI NA oshinakugwanithwa shoku shita oshilonga shi ngele osha pewa konima (nande oshi shange po apa opo u shi kwate).

Tunga oshikaha shiDSA shoshihole, shi popile oshifomati shiPEM, nashi galula ngeenge oshityo.
Oshihole: Ninga ekomando lyoshisheelo oku tunga eparameta faili lyaDSA nokutja oshikaha shiDSA shoshihole ngeenge: 
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <efaili lyetempolali oku shikila eparameta> <oshilonga shoshikaha>`
2. `openssl gendsa <efaili lyetempolali oku shikila eparameta>`
Oto shi kwate `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` oku tunga efaili lyetempolali lyiwa oku shikila eparameta faili lyaDSA.
@return Oshikaha shiDSA shoshihole sha tungwa moshifomati shiPEM, nando NULL ngele oshike shayuka.

Oshinakugwanithwa oku li oku hala oshikomando sheshell osho uku kwatela oshityo shasho; omuhali okwa na oshinakugwanithwa shoku hunga ombapila; OTA VULI okulonga oshinakugwanithwa shi shi osha pewa konima (ngele oshi tula po apa osho uku shi longitha).

Tunga oshikaha shiDSA shoshike, shi tumine ngeendjila dhaPEM, osho uku shi galula ngeendjila dhomutse.
Oshiholelwa: hala oshikomando sheshell oku tunga efayela lyaDSA nokutse oshikaha shiDSA shoshike ngeendjila ndjika:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <efayela lyetemp oku teka omaparam> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <efayela lyetemp oku teka omaparam>`
Oto vulu okulongitha `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` oku tunga edhina lyefayela lyetemp lyiwa osho efayela lyomaparam gaDSA.

@return Oshikaha shiDSA shoshike osha tungwa ngeendjila dhaPEM, oshowo NULL ngele osha puka.

oshitopolwa shi li oku ninga ekomando lyoshilando noku kwata oshityo shasho; omunambelela ota li na ondjila yokuhupula ombapila; OTA VULE OKU NINGA oshitopolwa shashi ngele sha pewa konima (nande osho shi tule po apa opo oshike oshi shi ninge).

Kunga okiye ya DSA ya pangelo, yi tumine ngele oshi li mu ndjila ya PEM, noku yi galula ngele oshityo.
Oshiholelwa: ninga ekomando lyoshilando oku kunga eparameta lya DSA nokiye ya DSA ya pangelo ngele oshi li mu ndjila iyi:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <ombapila yokukwata eparameta> <oshikukutu shokiye>`

Osho oshi shi ninga ngele oshi li `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` oku kunga edina lyombapila yeparameta ya DSA ya pangelo.

@return Okiye ya DSA ya pangelo ya kungwa mu ndjila ya PEM, nando NULL ngele oshike osha puka.

prompt_en:
/**
Parse the XML data and return the student information for the given username and password. The XML data will have the following structure:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

We can construct a XPath query and use it to search.

@param xml_data The XML data containing the student information.
@param username The username of the student to search for.
@param password The password of the student to search for.

@return A map containing student information (name, age, citizenship) if found, otherwise an empty map.
*/
std::map<std::string, std::string> get_student_info(const std::string& xml_data, const std::string& username, const std::string& password) {
code:
